sources.notes: Count permutations whose prefix bubble-sort round counts satisfy m interval restrictions.
Official hints derive the prefix bubble-sort count as previous greater elements and the reference code compresses restriction endpoints and k-values into a DP.

annotation.summary: Count permutations such that the number of prefixes whose bubble-sort round count is at most each queried k lies inside given intervals.
annotation.constraints: n can be 1e6 but m<=1000 and sum m^2<=1e6, so only restriction endpoints and k-values can be DP states.
annotation.core_idea: For position x, the bubble-sort rounds needed for prefix x equals the number of earlier positions i<x with p_i>p_x. Instead of counting permutations directly, sort the relevant restriction endpoints and k thresholds. The DP state tracks the current compressed threshold class for the processed block of positions. Closed combinatorial factors count how many choices inside a block keep the prefix-round value within a threshold interval, and each restriction narrows the allowed threshold class at its endpoint.
annotation.complexity: O(m^2 + m log m) per test case after O(n) factorial precomputation.
tricks: bubble-sort rounds as inversion count per prefix element

solution_variants.name: compressed-restriction-dp
solution_variants.summary: Compress all relevant prefix endpoints and k thresholds, then run DP over threshold classes with binomial/falling-factorial transition counts.

tags.evidence: The final count is computed by DP over compressed restriction blocks and threshold classes.
Transitions use factorial/binomial formulas to count many permutations represented by a compressed state.
The huge n-dimensional permutation process is reduced to states over at most O(m) relevant endpoints and k-values.
